notes: commit=2f190303f8c978400f422b90ae666262ce5d8dc4; extraction_method=ast; doc_density_composite=80.0; params are name-only placeholders (annotation='Any') -- EXP-1 classifier tests selection only, not call-correctness.

tools.name: delete
tools.docstring:
Delete documents or wiki pages from the knowledge vault.

Provide a path to delete a single file, or a glob pattern to delete multiple.
Examples:
- `path="old-notes.md"` — delete a single file
- `path="/wiki/drafts/*"` — delete all files in a folder
- `path="/wiki/**"` — delete the entire wiki

Note: overview.md and log.md are structural pages and cannot be deleted.
Returns a list of deleted files. This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: guide
tools.docstring: Get started with LLM Wiki. Call this to understand how the knowledge vault works and see your available knowledge bases.

tools.name: lint
tools.docstring:
Run deterministic hygiene checks across a knowledge base.

Checks wiki frontmatter, tag/date index consistency, footnote hygiene, citation resolution, citation graph edges, dangling wiki links, orphan pages, uncited sources, and stale pages.

Use `path` to scope the run, e.g. `*`, `/wiki/**`, or `/wiki/concepts/*.md`.

tools.name: create_knowledge_base
tools.docstring:
Create a new knowledge base and scaffold starter overview/log pages.

Set kind='course' to create a course instead of a wiki — same structure, but the app renders lesson progress (mark-complete, current/locked lessons). Default 'wiki'.

In hosted mode this creates a separate knowledge base with a unique slug. In local MCP mode there is one workspace per server, so this returns the existing workspace if it has already been initialized.

tools.name: set_course_mode
tools.docstring: Convert an existing knowledge base into a course (kind='course') or back into a plain wiki (kind='wiki'). A course renders its pages as ordered lessons with progress tracking; a wiki is free-form. Reversible — this only changes how the app renders the knowledge base, never its content.

tools.name: list_knowledge_bases
tools.docstring:
List the user's knowledge bases with their names and slugs.

Every other tool takes a `knowledge_base` slug — call this first to discover the valid slugs, or whenever you need to confirm which knowledge bases exist.

tools.name: read
tools.docstring:
Read document content from the knowledge vault.

Accepts a single file path OR a glob pattern to batch-read multiple files:
- `path="notes.md"` — read one file
- `path="*.md"` — read all markdown files in root
- `path="/wiki/**"` — read all wiki pages
- `path="**/*.md"` — read all markdown files everywhere

Batch reads are the PREFERRED way to read multiple documents at once — use them generously.
Glob reads sample the first few pages from each document (including PDFs) up to a 120k char budget. This gives you a broad overview of an entire folder in one call. Read individual files for full content.

For PDFs and office docs, use `pages` to read specific page ranges (e.g. '1-50', '3', '10-30').
You can read up to 50+ pages in a single call — use wide ranges to avoid unnecessary round trips.
For spreadsheets, each sheet is a page (call without pages first to see sheet names).
Set `include_images=true` to include embedded images (e.g. figures in PDFs, standalone image files). Off by default to save context — enable when you need to see charts, diagrams, or photos.

When reading sources to compile wiki pages, note the filename and page ranges for citation.

tools.name: search
tools.docstring:
Browse or search the knowledge vault.

Sources (raw documents) live at `/`. Wiki pages (LLM-compiled) live at `/wiki/`.

Modes:
- list: browse files and folders
- search: keyword search across document content (searches chunks for precise results with page numbers)
- references: query the citation/link graph for a document

References mode examples:
- `search(mode="references", path="/wiki/concepts/scaling.md")` — what it cites + what links to it
- `search(mode="references", path="paper.pdf")` — which wiki pages cite this source
- `search(mode="references", query="uncited")` — sources with no wiki citations
- `search(mode="references", query="stale")` — pages flagged as potentially stale

Use `path` to scope: `*` for root, `/wiki/**` for wiki only, `*.pdf` for PDFs, etc.
Use `tags` to filter by document tags.

tools.name: create
tools.docstring:
Create a new wiki page, note, or asset in the knowledge vault.

Wiki pages should be created under `/wiki/` and should cite their sources using markdown footnotes (e.g. `[^1]: paper.pdf, p.3`). If markdown wiki content does not include YAML frontmatter, this tool adds it from `title`, `tags`, and `date_str`.

You can also create SVG diagrams and CSV data files as wiki assets:
- `create(path="/wiki/", title="architecture-diagram.svg", content="<svg>...</svg>", tags=["diagram"])`
- `create(path="/wiki/", title="data-table.csv", content="col1,col2\nval1,val2", tags=["data"])`
SVGs and other assets can be embedded in wiki pages via `![Architecture](architecture-diagram.svg)`

Rejects if the page already exists — use `overwrite=true` to replace, or use the `edit` tool to modify.

tools.name: edit
tools.docstring:
Replace exact text in an existing wiki page or note.

Works like find-and-replace: provide the exact text to find (`old_text`) and the replacement (`new_text`). The match must be unique — if multiple matches are found, provide more surrounding context to disambiguate.

Read the page first to see its current content before editing.

tools.name: append
tools.docstring:
Append content to the end of an existing wiki page or note.

Useful for adding new sections, log entries, or additional findings to a page without reading and rewriting the entire document.
